title: tax — model relationships
description: Four owned entities. TaxJurisdiction is the reference-data root; TaxRate + TaxExemption reference it. TaxCalculation is standalone (references invoice_lines from Wave 4 finance::invoice). Every outbound FK stays same-tenant per tenancy-columns.md §5. NO application_id / region_id / organization_id / scope_node_id on any owned row.

internal.description: Per-jurisdiction rate rows. RESTRICT on jurisdiction hard-delete (a jurisdiction with rates cannot be deleted; must archive rates first).
RESTRICT — rates anchor to their jurisdiction. A jurisdiction with rates cannot be deleted.
Exemptions issued by this jurisdiction. RESTRICT on jurisdiction hard-delete.
The jurisdiction that ISSUED the certificate. Distinct from the tax jurisdictions the exemption applies TO (which is inferred from customer address at calculation time).

outbound.description: Every tax rate is tenant-scoped per tenancy-columns.md §3.
tenant_id nullable — null = platform-wide reference data (ISO country + state jurisdictions). Cascade only fires for tenant-scoped custom jurisdictions.
Polymorphic customer: User, Athlete, Organization. Each type has its own tenant scoping; the observer validates cross-model tenant integrity.
The actor who verified the exemption. RESTRICT for audit-integrity — user hard-delete cannot orphan verification records.
Cascade fires AFTER MigrateCalculationsToArchiveOnTenantErased priority-20 hook migrates rows to tax_calculations_archive. Defensive fallback only.

The invoice line that was taxed. RESTRICT — an invoice line with a tax calculation cannot be hard-deleted; the invoice module's retention pipeline handles this via credit-memo flow. Nullable for real-time preview calculations (POST /calculate) which do NOT persist an invoice-line link.

inbound.description: Tax has one primary inbound consumer: the invoice module (Wave 4 peer). Every invoice_line references tax_calculation_id (nullable — populated after CalculateTaxForInvoiceLineJob succeeds). Failed calculations leave tax_calculation_id NULL, which blocks the invoice line from proceeding to payment.

examples.description: Set after successful tax computation. NULL = pending / failed / blocked.

inbound.note: coupon + membership + transaction modules do NOT hold direct FKs on tax rows. They interact with tax through invoice_lines (which is the singular tax-computation touchpoint).

morphs.description: Polymorphic customer of the exemption certificate. Each supported type is tenant-scoped. The observer validates cross-model tenant integrity.

notes: None of the four owned rows carry `application_id` — cascades through tenant_id → tenants.application_id per tenancy-columns.md §2. Only the 8 named rows carry application_id directly; tax_* is not among them.

None of the four owned rows carry `region_id`, `organization_id`, or `scope_node_id`. Tax is a domain-plane concern — region cascades through the tenant hierarchy (a tenant's default_region resolves via belongs-to chain, not a shortcut FK on tax rows).
TaxJurisdiction.tenant_id is NULLABLE — this is the only tax row that supports null tenant_id (platform-wide reference data). Every other row is strictly tenant-scoped.

TaxCalculation.invoice_line_id is NULLABLE — real-time preview calculations (POST /api/v1/tax/calculate) are recorded when metadata.persist_preview=true (rare) but usually just return the result without persisting.

The morph on TaxExemption.customer is deliberately loose — the module's discovery layer knows about specific types, but the base schema allows any (customer_type, customer_id) pair. This lets new subject types land without a schema change.

There is NO inverse hasMany from TaxCalculation to TaxRate — the rate is captured via tax_rate_snapshot jsonb, not via FK. Reason: rates change over time; the calculation-freeze pattern requires the SNAPSHOT to survive rate mutation (which would break FK integrity).
